label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The passage is about the relationship between economic growth and development, specifically looking at a World Bank study from 1974. The question asks: According to the study by the World Bank in 1974, economic growth in some backward countries brought ______. The options are: A) benefit to a third of their population B) benefit to two thirds of their population C) little benefit to their people D) no benefit at all to their people I need to find the part of the passage that mentions the World Bank study. Let me read through it again. Here's the relevant part: "A major study by the World Bank in 1974 concluded that it is now clear that more than decades of rapid growth in developing countries has been of little benefit to a third of their population." Wait, that seems a bit confusing. It says "little benefit to a third of their population," which might sound like the rest (two thirds) received some benefit. But the key phrase here is "has been of little benefit to a third of their population." So it's saying that a third received little benefit, but what about the rest? But let me check another part: "The World Bank study showed that increase in GNP per capita did not promise important improvements in such development indicators as nutrition, health, and education. Although GNP per capita did indeed rise, its benefit came down to only a small part of the population." So, the study showed that even though GNP per capita increased (which is economic growth), the benefits didn't reach most people. The rise was only for a small part of the population. Now let's look at the options again: A) benefit to a third of their population B) benefit to two thirds of their population C) little benefit to their people (this is vague, but maybe it refers to most people) D) no benefit at all to their people The passage says "little benefit to a third of
